assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md shows: 2.1.0 = YES (h2 0.4.5), 2.1.1 = YES (h2 0.4.5), 2.2.0 = NO (h2 0.4.8), 2.2.1 = NO (h2 0.4.8), 2.2.2 = NO (retag), 2.2.3 = NO (h2 0.4.9), 2.2.4 = NO (h2 0.4.9). Mixed Impact Summary confirms '2.1.x stream: ALL versions affected... 2.2.x stream: ALL versions NOT affected'.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md shows: 'Current: [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.2.0] -> Proposed: [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1]'. Rationale: 'RHTPA 2.2.0 removed: ships h2 0.4.8 (fixed version, not affected). RHTPA 2.1.1 added: ships h2 0.4.5 (affected, < 0.4.8).'

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 8 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md creates tasks for 2.1.x stream only (upstream target branch release/0.3.z, downstream repo rhtpa-release.0.3.z). Explicit section 'No Tasks for 2.2.x Stream' states: 'No remediation tasks are created for the 2.2.x stream because all versions in that stream already ship h2 >= 0.4.8'.

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md states: 'no stream suffix -- unscoped... This issue is treated as unscoped -- it covers all streams. Steps 3 and 8 will apply to all streams, and ALL versions across all streams are checked'. version-impact.md checks both 2.1.x and 2.2.x streams.

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 8 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md explicitly states: 'Cross-stream impact notice (Case B) is NOT generated because this issue is unscoped -- it covers all streams by definition. Case B applies only to scoped issues (those with a stream suffix).'
